name: Electronic Health Record-based Phenotyping Algorithm for Familial Hypercholesterolemia
description: This test case evaluates identification of patients with familial hypercholesterolemia (FH) based on elevated LDL cholesterol levels. The phenotype requires complex scoring using the Dutch Lipid Clinic Network criteria including LDL-C levels ≥190 mg/dL for cases, family history of hypercholesterolemia or premature ASCVD, personal history of premature cardiovascular disease, and physical examination findings like xanthomas. This represents a multi-component phenotype requiring both laboratory values and clinical assessments.
prompt: Find patients with elevated LDL cholesterol levels consistent with familial hypercholesterolemia, including those with LDL-C levels of 190 mg/dL or higher and associated cardiovascular risk factors.

required_codes.display: Low density lipoprotein cholesterol [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
